records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1056 - Umar ibn Uthayna reported from him (as) that he was asked about a man who experiences a nosebleed while in prayer and has already prayed part of it.
Imam (as) replied: "If the blood is on his right, left, or behind him, he should wash it without turning and continue with his prayer. If he cannot find water without turning, then he should repeat the prayer."  He also said: "Vomiting is similar to that."
records.arabicText: 1056 - وَ رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَرْعُفُ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ قَدْ صَلَّى بَعْضَ صَلاَتِهِ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ اَلْمَاءُ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ أَوْ عَنْ شِمَالِهِ أَوْ عَنْ خَلْفِهِ فَلْيَغْسِلْهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَلْتَفِتَ وَ لْيَبْنِ عَلَى صَلاَتِهِ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَجِدِ اَلْمَاءَ حَتَّى يَلْتَفِتَ فَلْيُعِدِ اَلصَّلاَةَ» قَالَ «وَ اَلْقَيْءُ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1056 - Umar ibn Uthayna reported from him (as) that he was asked about a man who experiences a nosebleed while in prayer and has already prayed part of it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "If the blood is on his right, left, or behind him, he should wash it without turning and continue with his prayer. If he cannot find water without turning, then he should repeat the prayer."  He also said: "Vomiting is similar to that."

records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1057 - In the narration of Abu Basir from Imam (as),
Imam (as) said: "If you speak or turn your face away from the qibla, then you should repeat the prayer."
records.arabicText: 1057 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنْ تَكَلَّمْتَ أَوْ صَرَفْتَ وَجْهَكَ عَنِ اَلْقِبْلَةِ فَأَعِدِ اَلصَّلاَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1057 - In the narration of Abu Basir from Imam (as),
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If you speak or turn your face away from the qibla, then you should repeat the prayer."

records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1058 - Abu Basir said to him:
"I hear someone sneeze, and I praise Allah (swt) and send blessings upon the Prophet (sw) while I am in prayer."
Imam (as) said: "Yes, even if there is a sea between you and your companion."
records.arabicText: 1058 - وَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ: أَسْمَعُ اَلْعَطْسَةَ فَأَحْمَدُ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى وَ أُصَلِّي عَلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ أَنَا فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ إِنْ كَانَ بَيْنَكَ وَ بَيْنَ صَاحِبِكَ اَلْيَمُّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1058 - Abu Basir said to him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I hear someone sneeze, and I praise Allah (swt) and send blessings upon the Prophet (sw) while I am in prayer."
Imam (as) said: "Yes, even if there is a sea between you and your companion."

records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1059 - Imam (as) said:
"If a blind person prays facing the wrong direction, and if it is during the time of the prayer, then he should repeat it. But if the time has passed, he does not need to repeat it."
records.arabicText: 1059 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْأَعْمَى إِذَا صَلَّى لِغَيْرِ اَلْقِبْلَةِ فَإِنْ كَانَ فِي وَقْتٍ فَلْيُعِدْ وَ إِنْ كَانَ قَدْ مَضَى اَلْوَقْتُ فَلاَ يُعِيدُ»
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1059 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a blind person prays facing the wrong direction, and if it is during the time of the prayer, then he should repeat it. But if the time has passed, he does not need to repeat it."

records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1060 - It is narrated from al-Fudayl ibn Yasar that He said:
"I said to Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as): 'I am praying and I feel a pain in my stomach, or a rumbling, or a sensation of something moving.'
Imam (as) said: 'Turn away (from prayer) and perform ablution, and then continue the prayer you have already performed, as long as you have not invalidated the prayer by deliberately speaking. If you spoke by mistake, there is no harm, and it is like the one who speaks in prayer by mistake.'
I asked: 'What if he turns his face away from the qibla?'
Imam (as) said: 'Yes, even if he turns his face away from the qibla.'"
records.arabicText: 1060 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَكُونُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَأَجِدُ غَمْزاً فِي بَطْنِي أَوْ أَزّاً أَوْ ضَرَبَاناً فَقَالَ «اِنْصَرِفْ وَ تَوَضَّأْ وَ اِبْنِ عَلَى مَا مَضَى مِنْ صَلاَتِكَ مَا لَمْ تَنْقُضِ اَلصَّلاَةَ بِالْكَلاَمِ مُتَعَمِّداً فَإِنْ تَكَلَّمْتَ نَاسِياً فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْكَ وَ هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ مَنْ تَكَلَّمَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ نَاسِياً» قُلْتُ وَ إِنْ قَلَبَ وَجْهَهُ عَنِ اَلْقِبْلَةِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ إِنْ قَلَبَ وَجْهَهُ عَنِ اَلْقِبْلَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1060 - It is narrated from al-Fudayl ibn Yasar that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I said to Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as): 'I am praying and I feel a pain in my stomach, or a rumbling, or a sensation of something moving.'
Imam (as) said: 'Turn away (from prayer) and perform ablution, and then continue the prayer you have already performed, as long as you have not invalidated the prayer by deliberately speaking. If you spoke by mistake, there is no harm, and it is like the one who speaks in prayer by mistake.'
I asked: 'What if he turns his face away from the qibla?'
Imam (as) said: 'Yes, even if he turns his face away from the qibla.'"

records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1061 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who feels discomfort in his stomach but is able to bear it. He asked whether the man should continue praying in that situation or not.
Imam (as) replied: "If he can tolerate the pain and does not fear it causing him to rush the prayer, then he should pray and be patient."
records.arabicText: 1061 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْغَمْزِ يُصِيبُ اَلرَّجُلَ فِي بَطْنِهِ وَ هُوَ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَصْبِرَ عَلَيْهِ أَ يُصَلِّي عَلَى تِلْكَ اَلْحَالَةِ أَمْ لاَ يُصَلِّي فَقَالَ «إِنِ اِحْتَمَلَ اَلصَّبْرَ وَ لَمْ يَخَفْ إِعْجَالاً عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فَلْيُصَلِّ وَ لْيَصْبِرْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1061 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who feels discomfort in his stomach but is able to bear it. He asked whether the man should continue praying in that situation or not.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he can tolerate the pain and does not fear it causing him to rush the prayer, then he should pray and be patient."

records.chapter: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1062 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Smiling does not invalidate the prayer, but loud laughter does. And smiling does not break the wudu (ablution)."
records.arabicText: 1062 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ يَقْطَعُ اَلتَّبَسُّمُ اَلصَّلاَةَ وَ يَقْطَعُهَا اَلْقَهْقَهَةُ وَ لاَ تَنْقُضُ اَلْوُضُوءَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1062 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Smiling does not invalidate the prayer, but loud laughter does. And smiling does not break the wudu (ablution)."

records.chapter: Greeting the Worshipper
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1063 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a person who greets others while in prayer.
Imam (as) said: "If a Muslim greets you while you are in prayer, respond to him by saying, ‘As-salamu ‘alayka,' and gesture with your finger."
records.arabicText: 1063 - سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَى اَلْقَوْمِ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَقَالَ «إِذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكَ مُسْلِمٌ وَ أَنْتَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَسَلِّمْ عَلَيْهِ تَقُولُ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْكَ وَ أَشِرْ بِإِصْبَعِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1063 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a person who greets others while in prayer.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a Muslim greets you while you are in prayer, respond to him by saying, ‘As-salamu ‘alayka,' and gesture with your finger."

records.chapter: Greeting the Worshipper
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1064 - Ammar al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about greeting someone while in prayer.
Imam (as) said: "If a man from the Muslims greets you while you are in prayer, respond to him within yourself and do not raise your voice”.
records.arabicText: 1064 - وَ سَأَلَ عَمَّارٌ اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلتَّسْلِيمِ عَلَى اَلْمُصَلِّي فَقَالَ «إِذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ أَنْتَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ فِيمَا بَيْنَكَ وَ بَيْنَ نَفْسِكَ وَ لاَ تَرْفَعْ صَوْتَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1064 - Ammar al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about greeting someone while in prayer.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man from the Muslims greets you while you are in prayer, respond to him within yourself and do not raise your voice”.

records.chapter: Greeting the Worshipper
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1065 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Imam (as) that He said:
"If someone greets a man while he is praying, he should respond to him quietly, as He (the greeter) said."
records.arabicText: 1065 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا سُلِّمَ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي يَرُدُّ عَلَيْهِ خَفِيّاً كَمَا قَالَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1065 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Imam (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If someone greets a man while he is praying, he should respond to him quietly, as He (the greeter) said."

records.chapter: Greeting the Worshipper
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1066 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Ammar greeted the Messenger of Allah (sw) while he was in prayer, and he responded to him."
Then Abu Ja'far (as) said: "Indeed, Salam (greeting) is one of the names of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."
records.arabicText: 1066 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «سَلَّمَ عَمَّارٌ عَلَى رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «إِنَّ اَلسَّلاَمَ اِسْمٌ مِنْ أَسْمَاءِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1066 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Ammar greeted the Messenger of Allah (sw) while he was in prayer, and he responded to him."
Then Abu Ja'far (as) said: "Indeed, Salam (greeting) is one of the names of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."

records.chapter: The One Praying Encounters Predatory Animals and Harmful Creatures and He Kills Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1071 - Sama’a ibn Mehran asked him about a man who is performing the obligatory prayer while standing and forgets his bag or belongings, fearing they might be lost or damaged.
Imam (as) said: "He may cut his prayer and secure his belongings."
Sama’a then said: "What if his animal breaks loose and he fears it might run away or cause harm?"
Imam (as) said: "It is no problem for him to cut his prayer, secure his animal, and then return to his prayer."
records.arabicText: 1071 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ بْنُ مِهْرَانَ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ قَائِماً فَيَنْسَى كِيسَهُ أَوْ مَتَاعَهُ يَخَافُ ضَيْعَتَهُ أَوْ هَلاَكَهُ قَالَ «يَقْطَعُ صَلاَتَهُ وَ يُحْرِزُ مَتَاعَهُ» قَالَ قُلْتُ فَتَفَلَّتُ عَلَيْهِ دَابَّتُهُ فَيَخَافُ أَنْ تَذْهَبَ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُ فِيهَا عَنَتٌ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَقْطَعَ صَلاَتَهُ وَ يُحْرِزَ وَ يَعُودَ إِلَى صَلاَتِهِ.

records.chapter: The One Praying Encounters Predatory Animals and Harmful Creatures and He Kills Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1068 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a person who is harmed by an animal while he is praying.
He (as) replied: "He should throw it away from him if he wishes, or bury it in the pebbles."
records.arabicText: 1068 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ تُؤْذِيهِ اَلدَّابَّةُ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي قَالَ «يُلْقِيهَا عَنْهُ إِنْ شَاءَ أَوْ يَدْفِنُهَا فِي اَلْحَصَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1068 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a person who is harmed by an animal while he is praying.
He (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should throw it away from him if he wishes, or bury it in the pebbles."

records.chapter: The One Praying Encounters Predatory Animals and Harmful Creatures and He Kills Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1069 - Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a person who scratches himself while in prayer. He (as) replied: "There is no harm."
records.arabicText: 1069 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْتَكُّ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1069 - Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a person who scratches himself while in prayer. He (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm."

records.chapter: The One Praying Encounters Predatory Animals and Harmful Creatures and He Kills Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1070 - He asked him about a person who kills a louse, a flea, a mosquito, or a fly while in prayer. Does this invalidate his prayer and wudu?
Imam (as) said: "No."
records.arabicText: 1070 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَقْتُلُ اَلْبَقَّةَ وَ اَلْبُرْغُوثَ وَ اَلْقَمْلَةَ وَ اَلذُّبَابَ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ أَ يَنْقُضُ ذَلِكَ صَلاَتَهُ وَ وُضُوءَهُ قَالَ «لاَ.

records.chapter: The One Praying Encounters Predatory Animals and Harmful Creatures and He Kills Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1072 - Ammar al-Sabati asked him about a man who is in prayer and sees a snake in front of him. Is it permissible for him to step forward and kill it?
Imam (as) said: "If there is just one step between him and the snake, then he may step forward and kill it; otherwise, he should not."
records.arabicText: 1072 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَمَّارٌ اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَيَرَى حَيَّةً بِحِيَالِهِ هَلْ يَجُوزُ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَنَاوَلَهَا وَ يَقْتُلَهَا قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ بَيْنَهَا وَ بَيْنَهُ خُطْوَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ فَلْيَخْطُ وَ يَقْتُلُهَا وَ إِلاَّ فَلاَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1072 - Ammar al-Sabati asked him about a man who is in prayer and sees a snake in front of him. Is it permissible for him to step forward and kill it?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If there is just one step between him and the snake, then he may step forward and kill it; otherwise, he should not."

records.chapter: The One Praying Encounters Predatory Animals and Harmful Creatures and He Kills Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1073 - Hariz narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"If you are in the obligatory prayer and you see one of your servants who has run away, or a debtor to whom you owe money, or a snake that you fear for your life, then break your prayer, follow your servant or debtor, and kill the snake."
records.arabicText: 1073 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا كُنْتَ فِي صَلاَةِ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ فَرَأَيْتَ غُلاَماً لَكَ قَدْ أَبَقَ أَوْ غَرِيماً لَكَ عَلَيْهِ مَالٌ أَوْ حَيَّةً تَتَخَوَّفُهَا عَلَى نَفْسِكَ فَاقْطَعِ اَلصَّلاَةَ وَ اِتْبَعْ غُلاَمَكَ أَوْ غَرِيمَكَ وَ اُقْتُلِ اَلْحَيَّةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1073 - Hariz narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you are in the obligatory prayer and you see one of your servants who has run away, or a debtor to whom you owe money, or a snake that you fear for your life, then break your prayer, follow your servant or debtor, and kill the snake."

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1075 - Al-Halabi asked him about a man who wishes to attend to a need while praying.
Imam (as) said: "He should gesture with his head, signal with his hand, and glorify (SubhanAllah (swt)). As for a woman, when She wants to attend to a need while praying, She should clap her hands”.
records.arabicText: 1075 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ: أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُرِيدُ اَلْحَاجَةَ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي فَقَالَ «يُومِئُ بِرَأْسِهِ وَ يُشِيرُ بِيَدِهِ وَ يُسَبِّحُ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِذَا أَرَادَتِ اَلْحَاجَةَ وَ هِيَ تُصَلِّي تُصَفِّقُ بِيَدَيْهَا.

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1074 - Abdullah ibn Abi Ya'fur narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a person who wishes to attend to a need while in prayer.
Imam (as) said: "He should gesture with his head and signal with his hand. As for a woman, when She wants to attend to a need, She should clap."
records.arabicText: 1074 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُرِيدُ اَلْحَاجَةَ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَقَالَ «يُومِئُ بِرَأْسِهِ وَ يُشِيرُ بِيَدِهِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِذَا أَرَادَتِ اَلْحَاجَةَ تُصَفِّقُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1074 - Abdullah ibn Abi Ya'fur narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a person who wishes to attend to a need while in prayer.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should gesture with his head and signal with his hand. As for a woman, when She wants to attend to a need, She should clap."

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1076 - Hanan ibn Sadeer asked him: "Can a man gesture while praying?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, the Prophet (sw) gestured in one of the mosques of the Ansar with a Muḥjan that he had with him."
Hanan said: "And I do not know except that it was the mosque of the Banu Abd al-Ashhal."
records.arabicText: 1076 - وَ سَأَلَهُ حَنَانُ بْنُ سَدِيرٍ: أَ يُومِئُ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ قَدْ أَوْمَأَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي مَسْجِدٍ مِنْ مَسَاجِدِ اَلْأَنْصَارِ بِمِحْجَنٍ كَانَ مَعَهُ » قَالَ حَنَانٌ وَ لاَ أَعْلَمُهُ إِلاَّ مَسْجِدَ بَنِي عَبْدِ اَلْأَشْهَلِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1076 - Hanan ibn Sadeer asked him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Can a man gesture while praying?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, the Prophet (sw) gestured in one of the mosques of the Ansar with a Muḥjan that he had with him."
Hanan said: "And I do not know except that it was the mosque of the Banu Abd al-Ashhal."

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1077 - Ammar ibn Musa asked him about a man who hears a sound at the door while he is in prayer and clears his throat to make his servant or family member hear, so that they come and look to see who is at the door. Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."
He also asked about a man and a woman who are in prayer and want something, whether it is permissible for them to say "SubhanAllah (swt)."
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and they can gesture to what they want. And a woman, when She wants something, can strike her thighs while in prayer."
records.arabicText:
1077 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَمَّارُ بْنُ مُوسَى: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَسْمَعُ صَوْتاً بِالْبَابِ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَيَتَنَحْنَحُ لِيُسْمِعَ جَارِيَتَهُ أَوْ أَهْلَهُ لِتَأْتِيَهُ فَيُشِيرَ إِلَيْهَا بِيَدِهِ لِيُعْلِمَهَا مَنْ بِالْبَابِ لِتَنْظُرَ مَنْ هُوَ فَقَالَ
«لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ» وَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ يَكُونَانِ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ وَ يُرِيدَانِ شَيْئاً أَ يَجُوزُ لَهُمَا أَنْ يَقُولاَ سُبْحَانَ اَللَّهِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ يُومِيَانِ إِلَى مَا يُرِيدَانِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِذَا أَرَادَتْ شَيْئاً ضَرَبَتْ عَلَى فَخِذَيْهَا وَ هِيَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1077 - Ammar ibn Musa asked him about a man who hears a sound at the door while he is in prayer and clears his throat to make his servant or family member hear, so that they come and look to see who is at the door.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."
He also asked about a man and a woman who are in prayer and want something, whether it is permissible for them to say "SubhanAllah (swt)."
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and they can gesture to what they want. And a woman, when She wants something, can strike her thighs while in prayer."

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1078 - Muhammad ibn Bajil, the brother of Ali ibn Bajil, narrated:
"I saw Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) praying, and a man passed by him while he was between the two prostrations. Abu Abdullah (as) threw a small stone at him, and the man came towards him."
records.arabicText:
1078 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَجِيلٍ أَخُو عَلِيِّ بْنِ بَجِيلٍ قَالَ:
رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُصَلِّي فَمَرَّ بِهِ رَجُلٌ وَ هُوَ بَيْنَ اَلسَّجْدَتَيْنِ فَرَمَاهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بِحَصَاةٍ فَأَقْبَلَ اَلرَّجُلُ إِلَيْهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1078 - Muhammad ibn Bajil, the brother of Ali ibn Bajil, narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) praying, and a man passed by him while he was between the two prostrations. Abu Abdullah (as) threw a small stone at him, and the man came towards him."

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1079 - It was narrated by Abu Zakariya al-Awra :
"I saw Abu al-Hasan (as) praying while standing, and beside him was an elderly man who wanted to stand up and had a stick with him. The man wanted to take the stick, so Abu al-Hasan (as), while still standing in his prayer, bent down and handed the man the stick. Then he returned to his place and resumed his prayer."
records.arabicText: 1079 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي زَكَرِيَّا اَلْأَعْوَرِ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُصَلِّي قَائِماً وَ إِلَى جَانِبِهِ رَجُلٌ كَبِيرٌ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَقُومَ وَ مَعَهُ عَصًا لَهُ فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يَتَنَاوَلَهَا فَانْحَطَّ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ هُوَ قَائِمٌ فِي صَلاَتِهِ فَنَاوَلَ اَلرَّجُلَ اَلْعَصَا ثُمَّ عَادَ إِلَى مَوْضِعِهِ إِلَى صَلاَتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1079 - It was narrated by Abu Zakariya al-Awra :
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw Abu al-Hasan (as) praying while standing, and beside him was an elderly man who wanted to stand up and had a stick with him. The man wanted to take the stick, so Abu al-Hasan (as), while still standing in his prayer, bent down and handed the man the stick. Then he returned to his place and resumed his prayer."

records.chapter: The One Praying Who Needs to Relieve Himself
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1080 - Abu Habib Najiyah said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"I have a mill where I grind sesame seeds, and I stand to pray, knowing that the servant is asleep. I strike the wall to wake him up."
Imam (as) said: "Yes, you are in the obedience of your Lord (azj), seeking your sustenance. There is no harm in that."
records.arabicText: 1080 - وَقَالَ أَبُو حَبِيبٍ نَاجِيَةُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: إِنَّ لِي رَحًى أَطْحَنُ فِيهَا اَلسِّمْسِمَ فَأَقُومُ وَ أُصَلِّي وَ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اَلْغُلاَمَ نَائِمٌ فَأَضْرِبُ اَلْحَائِطَ لِأُوقِظَهُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ أَنْتَ فِي طَاعَةِ رَبِّكَ تَطْلُبُ رِزْقَكَ لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1080 - Abu Habib Najiyah said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I have a mill where I grind sesame seeds, and I stand to pray, knowing that the servant is asleep. I strike the wall to wake him up."
Imam (as) said: "Yes, you are in the obedience of your Lord (azj), seeking your sustenance. There is no harm in that."

records.chapter: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
There is no call to prayer (adhan) or iqama for a woman, nor is there a Jumu’ah prayer (Jumu'ah) or congregational prayer (Jama'ah) for her.
When a woman stands in prayer, She gathers her feet together and does not spread them apart, placing her hands on her chest at the location of her breasts.
When She bows, She places her hands on her knees, on her thighs, so as not to bend too much and cause her back to rise.
When She goes to prostrate, She sits first and then prostrates, pressing her face to the ground and placing her arms on the ground.
When She rises to stand, She lifts her head from the prostration and sits on her buttocks, not in the way a man would sit. Then, She rises to stand without raising her back and hips, moving smoothly.
When She sits for the tashahhud (testification), She raises her legs and brings her thighs together. A free woman (Hura) does not pray except with a veil (niqab), while a slave woman (ama) prays without a veil.
records.arabicText: ليس على المرأة أذان ولا إقامة ولا جمعة ولا جماعة. وإذا قامت المرأة في صلاتها جمعت بين قدميها ولم تفرج بينهما، ووضعت يديها على صدرها لمكان ثدييها، فإذا ركعت وضعت يديها فوق ركبتيها على فخذيها لئلا تطأطأ كثيرا فترتفع عجيزتها، وإذا أرادت السجود جلست ثم سجدت لاطئة بالأرض وتضع ذراعيها في الأرض فإذا أرادت النهوض إلى القيام رفعت رأسها من السجود وجلست على أليتيها ليس كما يقعى الرجل، ثم نهضت إلى القيام من غير أن ترفع عجيزتها تنسل انسلالا ، وإذا قعدت للتشهد رفعت رجليها، وضمت فخذيها، والحرة لا تصلي إلا بقناع، والأمة تصلي بغير قناع.
